title: Public use of a generalist LLM chatbot for health queries

data_scope.user_type: consumer only (enterprise, educational and commercial accounts excluded)

core_conclusion: Analysis of 617,827 de-identified Microsoft Copilot health conversations reveals that nearly one in five involves personal symptom assessment; personal health queries spike in the evening and nighttime when traditional healthcare is least accessible; device type strongly predicts intent (mobile = personal health, desktop = professional/academic); and one in seven personal health queries concerns a dependent, positioning conversational AI as a caregiving tool.
why_it_matters: First large-scale validated intent taxonomy for health AI at population scale; quantifies after-hours unmet demand for healthcare; reveals caregiving use reframing target user population; provides device-specific design evidence; shows healthcare navigation queries as a signal of system friction.
why_nature: Population-scale real-world evidence from organic usage rather than survey or benchmark; directly policy- and safety-relevant findings; novel validated taxonomy methodology; establishes a longitudinal baseline for tracking health AI behavior change over time.
added_value_to_my_research: Diurnal intent patterns as demand proxy for after-hours primary-care deficit — overlays onto 2SFCA accessibility surfaces to identify digital access gaps
Taxonomy x trust x spatial access 3-layer framework linking health AI use to telehealth digital divides
Intent-stratified response quality evaluation using SHAP attribution for explainable health AI auditing
Intent-stratified fairness audit framework testing language/regional bias across 12 health intent categories
AI navigation query density as a new spatial indicator of healthcare system friction complementary to 2SFCA supply-demand ratios

related.title: A community-codesigned LLM-powered chatbot for primary care: a randomized controlled trial
related.relation: RCT of LLM chatbot in primary-care-constrained settings — demonstrates demand for AI health assistance where access is limited

related.title: A global survey on trust, digital health literacy and health information quality
related.relation: User trust and digital health literacy as moderators of health AI intent — explains why and how people engage with health AI

related.title: Access to care affects electronic health record reliability and AI-driven disease prediction
related.relation: Healthcare access disparities shape AI data quality — triangulates with chatbot navigation queries as access-gap signal

related.title: Improving the quality of machine learning in health applications and clinical research
related.relation: Quality evaluation framework for health ML — needed to assess response quality stratified by the 12-intent taxonomy

related.title: Algorithmic fairness and bias mitigation for clinical machine learning with deep reinforcement learning
related.relation: Bias mitigation for clinical AI — applicable to fairness auditing across intent categories and language groups

figures.caption: Distribution of health intents across N=617,827 conversations — Health Information and Education is largest at 40.8%; personal symptom assessment is nearly 1 in 5
Mobile vs. desktop usage throughout the day — desktop predominates during working hours, mobile dominates evenings and nights
Intent distribution by platform — mobile shows 2x higher symptom queries (15.9% vs 6.9%); desktop shows 3x higher research support (16.9% vs 5.3%)
Temporal changes in intent relative to morning — emotional well-being rises from 3.3% (morning) to 5.2% (nighttime); symptom queries rise from 10.6% to 13.4%
Who the health query is about — 14.5% of symptom queries and 14.9% of condition queries concern a dependent (child, parent, partner)
